title: Census 2027: Another school teacher dies due to suspected heatstroke in Odisha - Odisha TV
full_text:
In a disturbing incident, a school teacher died on Friday shortly after returning home from census duty in Basudevpur block of Bhadrak district. The deceased has been identified as Balaram Bindhani, a primary school teacher, associated with a government school in Chandbali.
According to local sources, Balaram Bindhani was working at a primary school in Garadapur village in Chandbali and had been engaged in census-related duties. As usual, he had gone on duty today and returned home after completing his assignment.
However, shortly after reaching home, the man allegedly fell sick all of a sudden, alarming his family members. He was immediately rushed to a nearby health facility in Basudevpur, where attending doctors declared him ‘dead’ upon arrival, leaving his family and colleagues in shock.
While the exact cause of Balaram’s sudden illness and death remains unclear and further medical examination may provide more clarity, sources said he may have died due to suspected ‘heatstroke.
However, authorities are yet to issue an official statement regarding the circumstances of the death.
Meanwhile, locals have expressed condolences and demanded necessary support for the bereaved family. The fatal incident has sparked grief among the local community and the education fraternity.
Worth mentioning, earlier in another fatal incident on April 19, a school headmaster from Subarnapur district had allegedly died after returning from census duty, with the family members alleging that heatstroke caused his death.
The deceased teacher had been identified as Bansidhar Bhoi, a resident of Bamaraj village under Ullunda block. He was serving as the headmaster of Sunapali Primary School.
